title: SpaceInvite/set destroy returns forbidden for nonexistent IDs instead of notFound
description: SpaceInvite/set destroy handler blindly returns forbidden for every ID in the destroy list without checking existence first. RFC 8620 §7.1 requires notFound for objects that do not exist. Trigger: POST /jmap with SpaceInvite/set destroy:["nonexistent-id"] → {type:forbidden} instead of {type:notFound}.
